title: CEPI source updated: News
key_message: Monitored R&D/access source changed during the 6-hourly dashboard update.
details: Keep up-to-date on the latest announcements and updates from CEPI and our global partners.
relevance: Review the source page for changes relevant to Bundibugyo therapeutics, vaccines, diagnostics, manufacturing, access, or clinical trial readiness.

title: Automated R&D screening: vaccines, therapeutics, diagnostics and clinical trial readiness for Bundibugyo ebolavirus
source: Europe PMC / PubMed / WHO / CEPI screening plan
key_message: The dashboard is configured to separately track product-development evidence rather than mixing it with epidemiological research.
details: Screening covers vaccine platforms, monoclonal antibodies, antivirals, diagnostics, assays, animal models, trial readiness, compassionate use, and regulatory guidance.
relevance: Separates translational R&D from epidemiological analysis.
screening_query: (Bundibugyo OR ebolavirus) AND (vaccine OR therapeutic OR monoclonal antibody OR antiviral OR diagnostic OR trial OR animal model)
journal_scope: npj Vaccines, Vaccine, Lancet Infectious Diseases, NEJM, Nature Medicine, Science Translational Medicine, JID, EBioMedicine

title: Oxford Bundibugyo ebolavirus vaccine candidate receives CEPI backing
key_message: Oxford/SII ChAdOx1 Bundibugyo candidate receives CEPI support and aims to move toward trials rapidly.
details: Track as a distinct ChAdOx1 platform candidate; relevant for manufacturing scale-up, dose availability and animal-data gap.
developer_or_sponsor: University of Oxford / Serum Institute of India / CEPI

title: Moderna and other groups get funding to develop Bundibugyo Ebola vaccine
key_message: CEPI funding package includes Moderna mRNA vaccine development, Oxford/SII and IAVI candidates.
details: Useful as a rapidly updated secondary source that consolidates funder, developer and amount details; verify against CEPI/Oxford/IAVI primary pages.

title: Merck discusses molnupiravir use in Ebola Bundibugyo response
key_message: Merck reported discussions with global health authorities about possible use of molnupiravir in the Bundibugyo outbreak response.
details: Track as a hypothesis-generating therapeutic item only; use would require expert guidance, safety review, pregnancy contraindication considerations, and trial or protocol context.
relevance: Potential antiviral candidate discussion for current outbreak.

title: CEPI fast-tracks three Bundibugyo ebolavirus vaccine candidates
key_message: CEPI is backing IAVI, Moderna and Oxford/SII vaccine candidates as a portfolio approach for Bundibugyo ebolavirus.
details: High-priority R&D item: track platform, dose availability, animal data, clinical trial timing, manufacturing and access commitments.
relevance: Directly relevant to vaccine development and trial readiness.

title: WHO emergency guidance on use of licensed Ebola virus vaccine during Bundibugyo outbreaks
key_message: WHO published emergency guidance on whether/how licensed Zaire ebolavirus vaccine should be considered during BVD outbreaks.
details: Critical policy item because Ervebo is licensed for Zaire ebolavirus disease, not Bundibugyo. The dashboard should track whether use is recommended only in research settings or not recommended for outbreak control.

title: WHO Technical Advisory Group on therapeutics prioritization for Bundibugyo virus disease
key_message: WHO TAG report prioritizes candidate therapeutics and trial considerations for BVD.
details: Extract products such as monoclonal antibodies and antivirals, endpoints, ethical criteria and operational feasibility. This is a core source for a therapeutics matrix.

title: WHO Technical Advisory Group on candidate vaccine prioritization: meeting report, 19 and 25 May 2026
key_message: WHO TAG-CVP assessed candidate vaccines for Bundibugyo virus disease.
details: Use to update candidate ranking, evidence gaps, expected timelines and conditions for field efficacy assessment.

title: Experts convened by WHO advise on candidate treatments and vaccines for BVD
key_message: WHO convened expert groups to assess potential vaccines and therapeutics for BVD prevention and treatment.
details: This is the highest-level public summary of R&D recommendations and should be presented prominently in the dashboard.

title: Cross-reactive Bundibugyo antibody responses after licensed Ebola vaccines
key_message: Preprint addresses cross-reactive antibody responses to Bundibugyo after licensed Ebola vaccines.
details: Important for the cross-protection question; interpret cautiously because it is a preprint and immunogenicity may not equal clinical protection.

title: CDC COCA call: Ebola Bundibugyo virus
key_message: CDC slides summarize clinical recognition, infection control, testing and lack of FDA-licensed Bundibugyo vaccine.
details: Useful for importation preparedness, differential diagnosis, laboratory biosafety and clinical consultation pathways.

title: Statement on vaccine efforts relating to the Bundibugyo Ebolavirus outbreak in the DRC
key_message: Oxford Vaccine Group is working with Oxford CBF and Serum Institute to produce and scale ChAdOx1 BDBV candidate doses.
details: Candidate-specific source for dashboard; later superseded by CEPI backing but remains useful for historical tracking.

title: Bundibugyo, the rare virus causing a deadly new Ebola outbreak, has no vaccine yet
key_message: Explains current vaccine gap and why existing Ebola vaccine assets may not directly solve BVD.
details: Useful for communication and background; not a primary research source.

title: What Bundibugyo Ebola vaccines and treatments are under development
key_message: Summarizes candidates including rVSV/BDBV-GP, ChAdOx1 Bundibugyo, mRNA vaccine, MBP134, maftivimab, remdesivir and obeldesivir.
details: Good secondary source to seed candidate matrices; primary verification is needed for each product.

title: Vaccine experts debate options to combat outbreak of unusual Ebola strain
key_message: Science coverage discusses debate around candidate vaccine options and unusual strain context.
details: Useful to capture scientific uncertainty and expert debate; should not be used as the sole source for candidate prioritization.

title: Confirming ERVEBO Vaccination to Support Ebola Virus Disease Epidemiology
key_message: Article discusses ERVEBO vaccination confirmation and implications for Ebola epidemiology.
details: Not Bundibugyo-specific, but relevant for vaccine documentation, serology and outbreak analytics if ring vaccination or research vaccination is considered.
relevance: Vaccine exposure ascertainment and epidemiological methods.
